volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 1191 - Section 1275)

sections.label: Section 1191
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν τὸν Οἴτης Ζηνὸς ὕψιστον πάγον ;
sections.translation: Good , then do you know the summit of Oeta , Zeus ’ s sacred mountain ?

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original: οἶδʼ , ὡς θυτήρ γε πολλὰ δὴ σταθεὶς ἄνω .
sections.translation: I know it . I have often stood on that height to sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 1193
sections.original: ἐνταῦθά νυν χρὴ τοὐμὸν ἐξάραντά σε
sections.translation: Then , you must carry my body there after raising it up in your own hands , aided by as many of our friends as you require ;

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: πολλὴν μὲν ὕλην τῆς βαθυρρίζου δρυὸς
sections.translation:
and when you have cut many a branch from the deep-rooted oak and chopped down many a sturdy wild-olive , you must lay my body on them and with a flaming pine-torch burn it .
And let no tear of mourning show itself there .

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀστένακτος κἀδάκρυτος , εἴπερ εἶ
sections.translation: No , do this without laments or tears , if you are indeed my son . But if you fail to do this , even from the world below my curse and my wrath shall await you for ever .

sections.label: Section 1203
sections.original: οἴμοι , πάτερ , τί δʼ εἶπας ; οἷά μʼ εἴργασαι .
sections.translation: Ah , Father , what have you said ? What you have done to me !

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: ὁποῖα δραστέʼ ἐστίν · εἰ δὲ μή , πατρὸς
sections.translation: I have spoken that which you must perform . If you refuse ,

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: ἄλλου γενοῦ του μηδʼ ἐμὸς κληθῇς ἔτι .
sections.translation: then get yourself some other father , and be called my son no longer !

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.original: οἴμοι μάλʼ αὖθις , οἷά μʼ ἐκκαλεῖ , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: Ah , me ! What deeds you call on me to do , Father — that I should become your murderer , polluted with your blood !

sections.label: Section 1208
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ ἔγωγʼ , ἀλλʼ ὧν ἔχω παιώνιον
sections.translation: Not so , in truth , but healer of my sufferings , sole physician of my pain !

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ὑπαίθων σῶμʼ ἂν ἰῴμην τὸ σόν ;
sections.translation: And how , by inflaming your body , shall I heal it ?

sections.label: Section 1211
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ φοβεῖ πρὸς τοῦτο , τἄλλα γʼ ἔργασαι .
sections.translation: If your fear keeps you from this one task , at least perform the rest .

sections.label: Section 1212
sections.original: φορᾶς γέ τοι φθόνησις οὐ γενήσεται .
sections.translation: The service of carrying you will not be refused .

sections.label: Section 1213
sections.original: ἦ καὶ πυρᾶς πλήρωμα τῆς εἰρημένης ;
sections.translation: And the heaping of the pyre which I have ordered ?

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: ὅσον γʼ ἂν αὐτὸς μὴ ποτιψαύων χεροῖν ·
sections.translation: Yes , except that I will not touch fire to it with my own hands .

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ἄλλα πράξω κοὐ καμεῖ τοὐμὸν μέρος .
sections.translation: All else will I do , and you will have no hindrance on my part .

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀρκέσει καὶ ταῦτα · πρόσνειμαι δέ μοι
sections.translation: Well , that much will be enough — yet add one small favor to your large benefits .

sections.label: Section 1218
sections.original: εἰ καὶ μακρὰ κάρτʼ ἐστίν , ἐργασθήσεται .
sections.translation: Even if it is tremendously large , it will be done .

sections.label: Section 1219
sections.original: τὴν Εὐρυτείαν οἶσθα δῆτα παρθένον ;
sections.translation: Do you know , then , the maiden , daughter of Eurytus ?

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: Ἰόλην ἔλεξας , ὥς γʼ ἐπεικάζειν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: You mean Iole , I would guess .

sections.label: Section 1221
sections.original: ἔγνως . τοσοῦτον δή σʼ ἐπισκήπτω , τέκνον ·
sections.translation: You know her . Just this is the command that I impose upon you , my son : when I am dead , if you wish to show your piety by remembrance of your oath to your father , make this woman your wife and do not disobey your father .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: μηδʼ ἄλλος ἀνδρῶν τοῖς ἐμοῖς πλευροῖς ὁμοῦ
sections.translation: Let no other but you take her who has lain close at my side . You , my son , make that marriage-bond your own . Obey ; for although you were obedient in great affairs , your disobedience in small ones cancels the gratitude already won .

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: οἴμοι · τὸ μὲν νοσοῦντι θυμοῦσθαι κακόν ,
sections.translation: Ah , me , it is wrong to be angry with a sick man , but who could bear to see him have thoughts like these ?

sections.label: Section 1232
sections.original: ὡς ἐργασείων οὐδὲν ὧν λέγω θροεῖς .
sections.translation: Your words show no willingness to do as I say .

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.original: τίς γάρ ποθʼ , ἥ μοι μητρὶ μὲν θανεῖν μόνη
sections.translation: Who on earth would , when she alone is to blame for my mother ’ s death , and for your present condition besides ?

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: τίς ταῦτʼ ἄν , ὅστις μὴ ʼξ ἀλαστόρων νοσοῖ ,
sections.translation: Who would choose to do so , unless he were infected by avenging fiends ? It would be better , Father , that I also die , rather than live united with those whom I most detest !

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: ἁνὴρ ὅδʼ , ὡς ἔοικεν , οὐ νεμεῖν ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: The man will render no due respect , it seems , to my dying prayer . No , be sure that the curse of the gods

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: μενεῖ σʼ ἀπιστήσαντα τοῖς ἐμοῖς λόγοις .
sections.translation: will await you for disobeying my commands .

sections.label: Section 1241
sections.original: ὤμοι , τάχʼ , ὡς ἔοικας , ὡς νοσεῖς φράσεις .
sections.translation: Ah , you will soon show , it seems , how diseased you are !

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: σὺ γάρ μʼ ἀπʼ εὐνασθέντος ἐκκινεῖς κακοῦ .
sections.translation: Yes , for you stir me from my slumbering plague .

sections.label: Section 1243
sections.original: δείλαιος , ὡς ἐς πολλὰ τἀπορεῖν ἔχω .
sections.translation: I am miserable ! I have no way out of so many dilemmas !

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ δικαιοῖς τοῦ φυτεύσαντος κλύειν .
sections.translation: Yes , since you think it wrong to obey your begetter .

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐκδιδαχθῶ δῆτα δυσσεβεῖν , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: But must I then learn to be unholy , Father ?

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: οὐ δυσσέβεια , τοὐμὸν εἰ τέρψεις κέαρ .
sections.translation: It is not unholy , if you gladden my heart .

sections.label: Section 1247
sections.original: πράσσειν ἄνωγας οὖν με πανδίκως τάδε ;
sections.translation: Do you , then , command me to do this as a clear duty ?

sections.label: Section 1248
sections.original: ἔγωγε · τούτων μάρτυρας καλῶ θεούς .
sections.translation: I do , and I call on the gods to bear me witness !

sections.label: Section 1249
sections.original: τοιγὰρ ποήσω κοὐκ ἀπώσομαι , τὸ σὸν
sections.translation: Then I will do it , and not refuse ,

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.original: θεοῖσι δεικνὺς ἔργον · οὐ γὰρ ἄν ποτε
sections.translation: — calling upon the gods to witness your deed . I can never be condemned for obeying you , Father .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: καλῶς τελευτᾷς , κἀπὶ τοῖσδε τὴν χάριν
sections.translation: Your words make a fair ending , and to them , my son , quickly add the gracious deed itself , so that you may place me on the pyre before any pain returns to tear or sting me .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: ἄγʼ ἐγκονεῖτʼ , αἴρεσθε · παῦλά τοι κακῶν
sections.translation: Come , make haste and lift me ! This , in truth , is rest from troubles , this is the end , the last end , of Heracles !

sections.label: Section 1257
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδὲν εἴργει σοὶ τελειοῦσθαι τάδε ,
sections.translation: Nothing hinders the fulfillment of your wish , since your commandment strongly compels me , Father .

sections.label: Section 1259
sections.original: ἄγε νυν , πρὶν τήνδʼ ἀνακινῆσαι
sections.translation: Come , then , before you awaken this

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: νόσον , ὦ ψυχὴ σκληρά , χάλυβος
sections.translation: plague , O my hardened soul , give me a bit of steel to bind my lips like stone to stone ! Stop any cry , since the task you accomplish , though by constraint , gives cause for joy .

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original: αἴρετʼ , ὀπαδοί , μεγάλην μὲν ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: Lift him , attendants !

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: τούτων θέμενοι συγγνωμοσύνην ,
sections.translation: Grant me ample forgiveness for this , but also recall the great cruelty of the gods in the deeds that are being done . They beget children and are hailed as fathers , and yet they can let such sufferings pass before their eyes .

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: τὰ μὲν οὖν μέλλοντʼ οὐδεὶς ἐφορᾷ ,
sections.translation: No man foresees the future ; the present is full of mourning for us , and of shame for the powers above , and indeed of hardship beyond compare for him who endures this disaster .

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: λείπου μηδὲ σύ , παρθένʼ , ἀπʼ οἴκων ,
sections.translation: And you , maiden , do not be left at the house . You have seen immense , shocking death , with sorrows great in number and strange . And in all of them there is nothing that is not Zeus .
